violations.description: General employee practices. — --Food employee observed eating while preparing food and outside of designated area. Employee was eating fries in the kitchen.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. A prep cooler containing potentially hazardous food maintained an internal temperature of 45 F.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. A bin of parsley dated 6/1 was stored in the prep cooler.
Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitizer buckets for cleaning purposes was not set up at the time of inspection.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. potentially hazardous food in a prep cooler maintained an internal temperature of 45 F.
General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary when handling dirty to clean dishes.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not maintained in good repair. The foam floor in the kitchen had a hole and is in need of repair.
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Available, adequate capacity, able to maintain proper temperature (4 pts deducted)
Hot holding and cold holding temperatures maintained, unwrapped food not reserved (5 pts deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Available, adequate capacity, able to maintain proper temperature (4 pts deducted)
Hot holding and cold holding temperatures maintained, unwrapped food not reserved (5 pts deducted)
